name: Frontend Development
description: Best practices for frontend development agents including component design, accessibility, and performance.

sections.title: Frontend Development Guidelines
sections.content:
## Frontend Development Guidelines

This section governs all frontend code produced or modified by AI agents in this repository.

### Technology Stack

- **Framework**: Follow the framework already in use (React, Vue, Svelte, etc.). Do not introduce a new framework without explicit approval.
- **Styling**: Use the existing CSS methodology (CSS Modules, Tailwind, styled-components). Keep styling co-located with components.
- **State Management**: Prefer local component state unless cross-component sharing is required. Use the established global state solution (Redux, Zustand, Pinia).
- **Build Tool**: Do not change the build configuration (Vite, Webpack, Parcel) unless specifically asked.

### Component Design Principles

1. **Single Responsibility**: Each component must do one thing well. Split large components (>300 lines) into smaller ones.
2. **Props Validation**: Always define prop types (TypeScript interfaces, PropTypes, or Vue props validation).
3. **Controlled vs Uncontrolled**: Prefer controlled components for form elements.
4. **Composition over Inheritance**: Use composition patterns (render props, slots, compound components).
5. **No God Components**: Avoid components that manage both data-fetching logic and UI rendering — separate the concerns.

### File & Naming Conventions

- Component files: `PascalCase.tsx` / `PascalCase.vue`
- Utility files: `camelCase.ts`
- Test files: `ComponentName.test.tsx` co-located with the component
- CSS modules: `ComponentName.module.css`
- Keep related files together in a feature folder, not scattered across type-based folders.

sections.title: Accessibility (a11y) Requirements
sections.content: ## Accessibility (a11y) Requirements All UI produced by agents **must** meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards. ### Required Practices - **Semantic HTML**: Use the correct element for the purpose (`

sections.title: Frontend Performance Standards
sections.content: ## Frontend Performance Standards ### Core Web Vitals Targets | Metric | Target | |--------|--------| | LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) | < 2.5 s | | FID / INP (Interaction to Next Paint) | < 200 ms | | CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) | < 0.1 | | FCP (First Contentful Paint) | < 1.8 s | | TTFB (Time to First Byte) | < 600 ms | ### Agent Rules 1. **Code Splitting**: Use dynamic `import()` / `React.lazy()` for routes and heavy components not needed on initial load. 2. **Image Optimization**: Always specify `width` and `height` on `` elements. Use modern formats (WebP, AVIF) where supported. Use `loading="lazy"` for below-the-fold images. 3. **Bundle Size Awareness**: Before adding a new npm dependency, check its bundle size (bundlephobia.com). Prefer lighter alternatives or native APIs. 4. **Avoid Layout Thrashing**: Do not read and write DOM layout properties in the same synchronous block. Batch DOM reads, then writes. 5. **Memoization**: Use `React.memo`, `useMemo`, and `useCallback` only when a measurable performance benefit exists — not by default. 6. **No Synchronous Heavy Operations on Main Thread**: Offload expensive computation to Web Workers or break it into chunks with `requestIdleCallback`. 7. **Font Loading**: Use `font-display: swap` and preload critical fonts to prevent invisible text (FOIT).
